title: Pipeline Architecture
summary: Explains the pipeline concept: overlapping instruction execution stages, performance metrics (latency, throughput), separation registers, and clock frequency determination.
content_points: Pipeline: n stages; each stage uses different CPU resources; n instructions active simultaneously.
First instruction: n cycles; subsequent: 1 cycle each (ideal throughput = 1 instr/cycle).
Clock period determined by the slowest stage; more, simpler stages → higher clock.
Separation registers hold results between stages.
Intel pipeline evolution: Pentium III=10, Pentium IV Prescott=32 stages.
Superscalar: multiple ALUs for >1 instruction per cycle. Superpipeline: multiple parallel pipelines.
learning_objectives: Explain how a pipeline improves instruction throughput
Calculate ideal throughput of a pipelined processor
